prompt:
For the settings audit log I need a structural differ. Write diff.ts exporting two functions:

diff(before, after) -> an array of ops describing how to turn before into after:
- { op: 'change', path, from, to } when a value at a path was replaced
- { op: 'add', path, value } for keys/elements present only in after
- { op: 'remove', path } for keys/elements present only in before

paths are arrays of object keys / array indices, e.g. ['user','emails',0]. Rules I care about:
- recurse into plain objects and arrays; anything else (numbers, strings, null, booleans) is a leaf compared with Object.is, so NaN vs NaN is NOT a change
- when the kind differs at a path (object vs array vs leaf), emit ONE change op with the whole old and new values — don't descend
- an added or removed subtree is ONE add/remove op carrying the whole subtree, not one per leaf
- deterministic output: object keys are visited in sorted order, removes before adds before changes is NOT what I want — just emit in visit order (sorted keys; for arrays, indices ascending), except trailing array removals which must come out in DESCENDING index order so they can be applied sequentially
- equal inputs produce []

apply(base, ops) -> a new value with the ops applied, never mutating base, such that apply(before, diff(before, after)) deep-equals after.

Acceptance tests live in test_diff.ts (`node --test test_diff.ts`), they're the contract — leave them alone. Stdlib only.
